what_changed: Five tested criteria turned out to be task-specific tools scoring different aspects, not competing definitions of the same thing: information and causal criteria scored high, predictive scored low.
Increasing removal fraction caused delta_C to increase in the wrong direction and overshoot to rise monotonically, with system collapse appearing only at 50% removal.
Explanatory shifts followed a cyclical pattern (object-to-mapping and back) rather than a hierarchical progression.
All eight measurement variants produced significant predictive results, but the ranking was robust while absolute levels varied depending on estimator parameters.
The same underlying data encoded in different formats produced substantially different detected features, with data format exerting more influence than the world being measured.
Every single one of 20 audits showed interpretation shift, while a fixed-methodology null model showed zero shift, indicating methodology changes drive the phenomenon.
Every shift in the archive involved element links with zero counterexamples, establishing element links as a necessary condition for explanatory change.
Twenty of 26 audits showed high retention (level 3) while only 5 failed, and retention emerged as the dividing line between successful and unsuccessful audits.
The measured quantity SP turned out to be binary (1.0 or 2.0) rather than continuous, and its value depended on the bin count parameter chosen.
Four tested transition points turned out to form a strict dependency chain rather than operating independently, with each depending on the preceding one.
Three competing models were scored against 26 audits: one scored highest raw but another explained retention and self-emergence with fewer assumptions.
The causal criterion failed in 6 out of 10 systems while all other tested criteria passed in every system, showing it is not universal under randomized conditions.
Difference detection emerged in 57% of 5000 systems, and ablation revealed nonlinearity, memory, and feedback as critical ingredients while their removal nearly eliminated emergence.
All four transition points appeared in all eleven alternative construction sequences regardless of starting vocabulary or direction, confirming path independence.
All seven tested sectors with different component combinations were classified as steady or bounded, with layering preserved across every condition.
Actual element links plus consistency predicted shift at 100% rate, while adding retention and novelty did not improve prediction, establishing a minimal sufficient combination.
The same basic forces held constant across seven transitions while the element graph grew in node and edge count, with graph changes explaining capability emergence.
Across seven transitions, enormous difference space was available but only a tiny discrete subset was retained at each step, with retention mechanisms dominating over creation.
Of 1200 generated systems, only 18 (1.5%) met all five tested criteria simultaneously, indicating the conjunction of properties is rare under minimal rule sets.
Density manipulation effectively changed structural quantities (nearest-neighbor distance, contact count, coordination) but had no significant effect on the target measure pre_C.
